title: GoThru Photographer API Go Project Report

issues.display_title: Issue #37: Update API Update Photographer Profile
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 29/06/26

✅ Implementation Complete :

Objective: Allow Basic-account subscriptions to update the website profile field.

  • Updated Eligibility Check:
    In src/action/v1/photographers/updatePhotographerProfile.go, identified 2 and 7 as the Basic subscription IDs. Added BASIC_SUBSCRIPTIONS = []int{2, 7} and included them alongside PRO_SUBSCRIPTIONS in the eligibility validation for the website field.
  • Updated Error Message:
    Adjusted the error message to explicitly state: "website is only available for Basic, Pro and Agency subscriptions" when a user lacks the required subscription level.
  • Adjusted Controller Handling:
    In src/controllers/v1/photographers/updatePhotographerProfile.go, updated the error string matching to "only available for Basic" so that the API correctly maps the new error to a 403 Forbidden status code for free/unauthorized users.
  • Verified Build:
    Successfully compiled the project (go build ./...) without errors.

Description

  • Allowed for Basic Account subscription
  • Route: PATCH /v1/photographers/:gothruUserId/profile

Relax the subscription gate on the update-profile endpoint so Basic accounts are also allowed (currently a capability here is restricted to Pro/Agency).

Current behavior (to change)

In src/action/v1/photographers/updatePhotographerProfile.go (lines ~87-120), the website field is restricted to Pro/Agency subscriptions and returns 403 for anyone else:

var PRO_SUBSCRIPTIONS = []int{1, 5, 6, 8}
...
if req.Website != nil && webVal != "" {
    // looks up user subscription
    if !eligible { // subscription not in PRO_SUBSCRIPTIONS
        return nil, errors.New("website is only available for Pro and Agency subscriptions")
    }
    updates["website"] = webVal
}

The controller maps that error to 403 (src/controllers/v1/photographers/updatePhotographerProfile.go).

Proposed

  • Allow Basic-account subscriptions to set the gated field(s) (e.g. website) on profile update — i.e. include Basic subscription ids in the eligibility check, or remove the Pro/Agency-only restriction per product intent.
  • Confirm which field(s) this applies to (the only sub-gated one today is website) and the exact set of subscription ids that count as "Basic".
  • Keep all other validation (slug uniqueness, startDate format, services/availabilities) unchanged.

Acceptance criteria

  • A Basic-account photographer can save the previously Pro/Agency-only field via PATCH /v1/photographers/:gothruUserId/profile without a 403.
  • Pro/Agency accounts continue to work unchanged.
  • Subscription→eligibility mapping documented (which ids = Basic / Pro / Agency).
  • Detail / list / dashboard caches still invalidated on save (existing behavior).

Relates to the profile save endpoint reviewed in this conversation.
